commit.message:
Fix AttributeError in `__repr__` for unprepared association types (#29)

`__repr__` on any association type (`RelationCollection`, `RelationSet`,
`RelationMap`, `TypedRelationMap`) raises `AttributeError` when called
before `prepare()` is invoked, since `__args__` (and `__typed_dict__`)
are instance attributes set only during `prepare()` or pydantic
validation — not in `__init__`.

```python
# Raises AttributeError: 'RelationCollection' object has no attribute '__args__'
repr(RelationCollection())
```

## Changes

- **`RelationCollection.__repr__`** / **`RelationSet.__repr__`**: use
`getattr(self, "__args__", None)` with `len` guard; fall back to `"?"`
when unset
- **`RelationMap.__repr__`**: same, requiring `len(args) >= 2` to safely
access both key and value types
- **`TypedRelationMap.__repr__`**: use `getattr(self, "__typed_dict__",
None)` with `None` guard; fall back to `"?"`
- Added `test_repr_unprepared` tests across all four association test
files to cover the pre-`prepare()` code path

commit.message:
Merge docs versioning into deploy workflow (#422)

* Initial plan

* fix: trigger version-docs workflow explicitly from release workflow

The release.yml creates GitHub Releases using GITHUB_TOKEN, whose
events do not trigger other workflow runs (known GitHub Actions
limitation).  This meant version-docs.yml (triggered by release:
published) was never invoked.

Fix by:
1. Adding workflow_dispatch trigger with tag input to version-docs.yml
2. Adding explicit gh workflow run step in release.yml to dispatch
   version-docs.yml after creating the release
3. Adding actions:write permission to release.yml for the dispatch

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: merge docs versioning into deploy workflow

Consolidate version-docs.yml into docs.yml so versioning and
deployment happen in a single workflow run.  When dispatched with
a release tag (from release.yml), docs.yml now:
1. Creates the Docusaurus version snapshot
2. Builds the site (including the new version)
3. Commits the snapshot files to main
4. Deploys to fiestaboard.github.io

This eliminates the 3-workflow chain (release → version-docs → docs)
in favour of a 2-step chain (release → docs).

Changes:
- docs.yml: add optional tag input, version snapshot steps, contents:write
- release.yml: dispatch docs.yml instead of version-docs.yml
- Delete version-docs.yml (logic merged into docs.yml)

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: auto-update for distributed workers (--auto-update)

Workers periodically check git for new commits (default every 60s).
When an update is detected, the worker finishes its current job,
releases leases, runs git pull --ff-only, and re-execs the process
with the same arguments. Gracefully degrades if not in a git repo.

Usage: imganalyzer run-distributed-worker --auto-update [--auto-update-interval 60]

* Revert removal of temporary muting during frame-forward stepping

Reverts commit 0155bae. The temporary muting suppresses the audio
blip from the play()/pause() cycle used by requestVideoFrameCallback
for frame-forward stepping on video elements.

Co-authored-by: cjmalloy <1704049+cjmalloy@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix keyboard shortcuts stopping after click/fullscreen: dual listener approach

Use @HostListener on viewer (for when viewer has focus) plus capture-phase
keydown listeners directly on video/audio elements (for when media element
or its shadow DOM controls have focus, including fullscreen).

Co-authored-by: cjmalloy <1704049+cjmalloy@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use tracked ViewChild references in HostListener with querySelector fallback

Co-authored-by: cjmalloy <1704049+cjmalloy@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix keyboard shortcuts in fullscreen with document-level capture listener

Add a document-level capture-phase keydown listener that only fires when
document.fullscreenElement is our video. In native fullscreen, events may
not propagate through the video element's regular event path, but they
always propagate through the document during capture phase. The guard
check (fullscreenElement === video && !defaultPrevented) is O(1) so adds
negligible overhead.

### src/video_creator.py — Modern neon subtitles + double-subtitle fix
- Remove duplicate top subtitle: captions now cover the FULL script
  (hook + body + CTA) in one consistent lower-third band (start_offset=0.0)
- New _make_glow_pill_image(): neon glow pill backgrounds via concentric
  rounded-rects — no scipy required
- New _adaptive_font_size(): 1-word bursts render up to 20% larger for
  maximum visual impact; longer bursts scale down to keep text on-screen
- New _hex_to_rgb() helper for colour conversion
- Neon 3-colour cycling palette: yellow → white → mint → yellow → pink → white
- Bold uppercase captions with thick stroke + drop-shadow for contrast
- Smooth 120ms crossfade between caption bursts
- Cinematic colour grade: 1.06× contrast S-curve + warm tint (fl_image step)
- Updated create_video() docstring to reflect full-script captioning

### config.py — New quality knobs
- SUBTITLE_GLOW=True, SUBTITLE_GLOW_COLOR='#00FFC8', SUBTITLE_GLOW_RADIUS=18
- SUBTITLE_ACCENT_COLOR='#FF4081', SUBTITLE_ALL_CAPS=True

- SUBTITLE_BG_OPACITY raised to 0.82, SUBTITLE_BG_CORNER_RADIUS to 28
- VIDEO_BITRATE raised to '16000k', AUDIO_BITRATE to '320k'

### src/scriptwriter.py — Richer script templates
- _HOOKS expanded from 20 to 33 templates: added emotional/storytelling,

- _BODIES expanded with 5 new templates: perspective, pattern, hidden-angle,

  high-energy descriptions (broadcast studio, mountain, laboratory, etc.)
- _TITLE_TEMPLATES expanded with 10+ new formats: number hooks, curiosity
  gap, emotional, and urgency-driven titles

### src/pipeline.py — Pass full script to video creator
- create_video() now receives script_text (full hook+body+CTA) instead of
  caption_text so every spoken word is captioned; removes the visual gap
  during the hook and eliminates any top-subtitle duplication

commit.message:
Add issue assignment strategy and GitHub Projects mandate to coordination protocols (#12)

No explicit mapping existed between issue type labels and agent
assignment, and there was no policy requiring trackable work to live in
a GitHub Project board.

### Issue Assignment Strategy
Added a triage lookup table under `Communication Conventions` in
`PROTOCOLS.md` mapping `type:*` label combinations to the responsible
agent and `owner:*` label:

| Label(s) | Agent | `owner:*` |
|---|---|---|
| `type:feature` + `type:backend` | dev-backend | `owner:dev-backend` |
| `type:feature` + `type:frontend` | dev-frontend | `owner:dev-frontend`
|
| `type:bug` | Owner of affected area | per area |
| `type:infra` | dev-infra | `owner:dev-infra` |
| `type:research` | biz-research | `owner:biz-research` |
| `type:cost` | cost-controller | `owner:cost-controller` |

Rules: one primary owner per issue (split if multi-agent), bugs assign
to area owner, unlabelled issues default to `owner:pm`.

Also extended the label table with four previously missing `type:*`
labels: `type:backend`, `type:frontend`, `type:infra`, `type:test`.

### GitHub Projects Mandate
Added a top-level `GitHub Projects Mandate` section enforcing that all
trackable items — issues, PRs, roadmap entries, effort estimates, sprint
goals — must be attached to a GitHub Project board. Key points:

- Both **PM** and **CEO** may create a project board when one doesn't
exist
- Default board template: Kanban with `Backlog / In Progress / In Review
/ Done`
- PM must add every issue/PR to the board at creation time; closing an
untracked issue requires retroactive addition
- Project board is the authoritative source of truth — progress must not
live only in Telegram or workspace files
